lines.speaker: Plato
lines.text: We can now build implications. But often we want to say that two things are true **together**.

lines.speaker: Plato
lines.text: To prove it, you must prove each half separately. That is `(and-intro N M)`: from a proof of $P$ at step $N$ and a proof of $Q$ at step $M$, form $P \\land Q$.

lines.speaker: Aristotle
lines.text: And to use a conjunction, you take it apart. `(and-elim-l N)` extracts the left half; `(and-elim-r N)` extracts the right.

lines.speaker: Plato
lines.text: A pair is exactly as strong as its two halves. No more, no less.
